overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted academic and professional presence across LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon. His content effectively bridges the gap between academic research, computational social science methods, and tool development, generating high-quality engagement from specialized audiences interested in disinformation and coordinated behavior studies.

contentThemes.description: Research updates, paper publications, and presentations focused on detecting coordinated link sharing and information operations.
Announcements regarding tool releases, such as CooRnet v2.0, focusing on computational methods.
Hiring posts for postdocs and calls for PhD students to join computational social science research initiatives.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's highly collaborative network for academic recruitment, as PhD and research opportunity posts generate exceptional repost-to-like ratios.
Cross-promote major software releases like CooRnet on LinkedIn to reach a broader audience of professionals and institutional stakeholders.
Incorporate visual elements, such as charts or tool interface screenshots, to further boost the engagement rates of technical posts on Mastodon.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits the highest relative engagement rate (6.14% average), with users highly eager to repost and share academic opportunities and newly published papers.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides a solid baseline of engagement from a technically minded audience, reacting favorably to specific software updates (CooRnet) and methodological discussions.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn serves as the primary driver for broad organizational reach, yielding the highest absolute engagement for major milestones like research grants and postdoctoral job openings.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
